problem_statement: Let M be the midpoint of side BC of triangle ABC. Prove that the median AM divides the triangle into two triangles ABM and ACM of equal area.

ABC is a triangle right-angled at A, and D is the foot of the altitude from A. The line joining the incenters of triangles ABD and ACD meets AB, AC at K, L. S and T are the areas of triangles ABC and AKL. Show that S >= 2 T.
On the sides of triangle ABC, equilateral triangles are erected outward with apexes A1, B1, C1 opposite to A, B, C. Prove that the lines AA1, BB1, CC1 are concurrent (at the first Fermat point).

In acute triangle ABC let D, E, F be the feet of the altitudes. Prove that the three points where the sides of the orthic triangle DEF meet the corresponding sides of ABC are collinear (the orthic axis).
Given three circles in the plane, each pair intersecting in two points, prove that the three radical axes (the lines through each pair's intersection points) are concurrent at the radical centre.
In acute triangle ABC let P be an interior point. Lines BP and CP meet AC and AB at E and F. Let D = AP ∩ EF and K the foot of the perpendicular from D to BC. Show that DK bisects angle EKF.
Let O, G and H be the circumcentre, centroid and orthocentre of triangle ABC. Prove that G lies on segment OH with OG : GH = 1 : 2, i.e. G = O + (H - O)/3.

Let A be a point on a circle with centre O, and let AB be a chord. Let t be the tangent to the circle at A. Prove that the angle between t and the chord AB equals the inscribed angle ACB for any point C on the major arc (tangent-chord angle).
In triangle ABC the internal bisectors of angles A, B, C meet the opposite sides; prove that the three angle bisectors are concurrent at the incentre I, which is equidistant from the three sides.

Given three circles of distinct radii, prove that the external centre of similitude of one pair and the internal centres of similitude of the other two pairs are collinear (the second theorem of d'Alembert / Monge).
Let ABCD be a convex quadrilateral with CD tangent to the circle on AB as diameter. Prove that AB is tangent to the circle on CD as diameter if and only if BC and AD are parallel.

In triangle ABC let D, E, F be the feet of the altitudes, M, N, P the midpoints of the sides, and X, Y, Z the midpoints of AH, BH, CH where H is the orthocentre. Prove that all nine points lie on one circle (the nine-point circle).

Prove that the circumcentre O and the orthocentre H of triangle ABC are isogonal conjugates, by showing that the reflection of line AO in the bisector of angle A is the altitude from A (it is perpendicular to BC).
Let the internal and external bisectors of angle A of triangle ABC meet BC at D and E, and let M be the midpoint of DE. Prove the Apollonius relation MD^2 = MB · MC.
The incircle of triangle ABC touches the sides BC, CA, AB at X, Y, Z. Prove that the lines AX, BY, CZ are concurrent (at the Gergonne point).

Two circles ω1, ω2 (centres O1, O2) intersect at A and B. X lies on ω2. Y is on ω1 with angle XBY = 90°. Let X' be the second intersection of line O1X with ω2 and K the second intersection of X'Y with ω2. Prove that X is the midpoint of arc AK.
Let P be a point on side AB of triangle ABC, and let the line through P parallel to BC meet AC at Q. Prove the basic proportionality (Thales) relation AP/PB = AQ/QC, i.e. AP·QC = PB·AQ.

Rectangles ABA1B2, BCB1C2, CAC1A2 lie outside triangle ABC. C' satisfies C'A1 perpendicular to A1C2 and C'B2 perpendicular to B2C1; A' and B' are defined analogously. Prove that lines AA', BB', CC' are concurrent.
Let ABCD be a rectangle and P any point in the plane. Prove the British flag theorem: PA^2 + PC^2 = PB^2 + PD^2.

Let the internal and external bisectors of angle A of triangle ABC meet the circumcircle again at M and M'. Prove that M and M' are diametrically opposite (MM' is a diameter), hence O is the midpoint of MM'.

Let the tangents to the circumcircle of triangle ABC at A, B, C form the tangential triangle with vertices TA (tangents at B,C), TB (tangents at C,A), TC (tangents at A,B). Prove that ATA, BTB, CTC are concurrent (at the symmedian point).
Let G be the centroid of triangle ABC. Prove that the three triangles GBC, GCA, GAB have equal area (each one third of the area of ABC).
Let ABC be an equilateral triangle and P a point on the arc BC of its circumcircle not containing A. Prove that PA = PB + PC.

Suppose ABCD is a parallelogram with angle DAC = 90 deg. Let H be the foot of the perpendicular from A to DC. Let P be a point on line AC such that PD is tangent to the circumcircle of triangle ABD. Prove that angle PBA = angle DBH.

Let A be one of the two intersection points of two unequal circles C1, C2 with centers O1, O2. One common tangent touches C1 at P1 and C2 at P2; the other touches C1 at Q1 and C2 at Q2. Let M1, M2 be the midpoints of P1Q1 and P2Q2. Prove that angle O1AO2 = angle M1AM2.

Let the incircle of triangle ABC touch BC at X, let X'' be the point of the incircle diametrically opposite X, and let X' be the point where the A-excircle touches BC. Prove that A, X'' and X' are collinear.
Let I be the incenter of triangle ABC. The incircle touches BC, CA, AB at K, L, M. The line through B parallel to MK meets lines LM and LK at R and S. Prove that angle RIS is acute.
Points A, B, C, D lie on a circle with AB a diameter and CD not. The tangents at C and D meet at P, and AC and BD meet at Q. Show that PQ is perpendicular to AB.

The incircle is inscribed in triangle ABC. Three tangents to the incircle are drawn, each parallel to one side, forming three smaller triangles, each with its own incircle. Determine the sum of areas of all four incircles.

Let P, Q be on side BC of acute triangle ABC with angle PAB = angle BCA and angle CAQ = angle ABC. M, N are on rays AP, AQ with P the midpoint of AM and Q the midpoint of AN. Prove that BM and CN meet on the circumcircle of ABC.

Let I and O be the incentre and circumcentre of triangle ABC, V the reflection of I in O (the Bevan point), and I_A the A-excentre. Prove that the Bevan circle (circumcircle of the excentral triangle) has radius 2R, i.e. VI_A equals the diameter AA' of the circumcircle.

predicted_construction_steps.name: I_A
predicted_construction_steps.label: I_A
predicted_construction_steps.command: Intersect(AngleBisector(C, A, B), PerpendicularLine(B, AngleBisector(A, B, C)))
